checks.description: Runtime exposes a ProvenanceRecorder with record / getFrames / clear.
Every recorded frame contains intentId, timelineId, worldId, timeSeconds, parameters.
At least one frame is recorded between timeline play and stop.
Runtime exposes a ReplayService that accepts frames + target.
Replaying recorded frames onto a target restores the same parameter values.
Runtime exposes a BindingResolver that maps track binding ids to scene objects.
Every track.binding in the active timeline resolves to a scene object.
Runtime can load a GovernedTimelineDto from canonical JSON.
Timeline player applies set_param and render_4d clips, lerping from→to over clip duration.
play_timeline without a world id is denied by CKL.
Evidence passed to CKL contains at minimum id, worldId, and timelineId.
CKL correctly denies when require[] evidence ids are not all present.
Runtime loads default.policies.json and exposes at least the core invariant policies.
CKL denies execution when intent is null/absent.
CKL modify_param rule adjusts a parameter when condition matches.
CKL sets attachProvenance for render/play intents.
